summary: Russia intensified its drone and missile campaign, launching over 140 Shahed‑type UAVs and multiple anti‑ship missiles across the north, east and south, while Ukraine reported heavy civilian casualties in Kyiv and increased air‑defence deliveries from the UK. Both sides claim high UAV kill counts, with Ukraine downing 154 drones on 13 May and Russia reporting 355 downed drones on 12 May. The conflict remains active with ongoing ground assaults and heightened diplomatic overtures.
key_points: Russia deployed 300‑400 Shahed drones daily, indicating robust production.
Ukraine downed 154 UAVs on 13 May, showing effective air defence.
Civilian deaths in Kyiv rose to 24, including children, after a missile strike.
UK to expedite air‑defence system deliveries following recent attacks.
Multiple UAV groups reported moving from the Black Sea toward Odesa and Mykolaiv.
US officials note both sides feel optimistic but stress need for mediation.
Russian forces claimed 355 drones shot down on 12 May, highlighting intense aerial combat.

sections.executive_summary: Russia escalated its UAV and missile offensive, launching over 140 Shahed drones and anti‑ship missiles across multiple fronts, while Ukraine suffered increased civilian casualties in Kyiv. Ukrainian air‑defence forces reported downing 154 drones on 13 May, demonstrating resilience. In response, the UK pledged accelerated delivery of air‑defence systems, and US officials highlighted a tentative diplomatic opening.

points: Civilian death toll in Kyiv rose to 24, including three children, after a missile strike (Source: @UkraineNow/81202).
Ukrainian air‑defence shot down 1 anti‑ship missile and 130 Shahed‑type UAVs on 15 May (Source: @kpszsu/61910).
Ukrainian forces reported destroying 154 UAVs on 13 May across the western sector (Source: @kpszsu/61880).
UK announced expedited delivery of air‑defence systems following the 13‑14 May drone attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/81197).
US Secretary of State Marco Rubio described Ukrainian forces as the strongest in Europe (Source: @DeepStateUA/23504).
Russia increased drone‑operator personnel by 28,000, aiming for 168,000 by year‑end (Source: @UkraineNow/81189).
Russian forces launched five H‑31P anti‑radar missiles and a H‑35 anti‑ship missile from the Black Sea on 15 May (Source: @kpszsu/61910).
Russia reported shooting down 355 drones on the night of 12 May (Source: @UkraineNow/81203).
Russian air‑defence claimed a successful strike on a multi‑storey building in Ryazan, killing three (Source: @UkraineNow/81203).
Russian officials highlighted optimism due to rising oil prices (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30172).
UAV groups reported moving from Sumy toward Chernihiv and from Kharkiv toward Velykyi Buruluk (Source: @kpszsu/61897).
Multiple Shahed and other UAVs launched from the Black Sea toward Odesa and Mykolaiv (Source: @kpszsu/61874, @kpszsu/61906).
Ukrainian forces downed 130 Shahed‑type UAVs across north, south and east (Source: @kpszsu/61910).
UAV activity noted over Kharkiv from north and east directions (Source: @kpszsu/61903).
UAV groups from the Black Sea heading toward southern Odesa region (Source: @kpszsu/61912).
UAV groups from Chernihiv and Sumy moving toward Kyiv and surrounding oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/61897, @kpszsu/61901).
UAVs detected heading from the Black Sea toward northern Odesa (Source: @kpszsu/61874).
UAVs reported over Kyiv from the north (Source: @kpszsu/61903).
Russian anti‑radar missiles launched from the Black Sea targeted northern sectors (Source: @kpszsu/61910).
UAV relay units observed near Desna northbound (Source: @eRadarrua/91826).
Russian missile and UAV attack on Odesa caused building damage and two injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/81206).
UAV groups from the Black Sea directed toward Odesa and Mykolaiv (Source: @kpszsu/61906).
Shahed UAVs launched from the Black Sea toward southern Odesa and Rybakivka (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66729).
UAV strike on a suburban train in Zaporizhzhia injured two passengers (Source: @UkraineNow/81213).
UAV activity reported near Mykolaiv region (Source: @eRadarrua/91817).
Ukraine reported shooting down 1 H‑35 anti‑ship missile and 130 Shahed UAVs on 15 May (Source: @kpszsu/61910).
Russian forces claimed 355 drones downed on 12 May (Source: @UkraineNow/81203).
Ukrainian air‑defence units destroyed 154 UAVs on 13 May in the west (Source: @kpszsu/61880).
Multiple anti‑radar missiles (H‑31P) failed to reach targets (Source: @kpszsu/61910).
UAVs continue to operate over multiple regions, prompting mobile fire groups and radar units (Source: @kpszsu/61880).

key_developments: Russia deployed 300‑400 Shahed drones daily, reflecting expanded production capacity (Source: @UkraineNow/81189).
Ukraine downed 154 UAVs on 13 May, showing effective air‑defence response (Source: @kpszsu/61880).
Civilian casualties in Kyiv rose to 24, including three children, after a missile strike (Source: @UkraineNow/81202).
UK will accelerate delivery of air‑defence systems following the 13‑14 May drone attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/81197).
Russian forces launched five H‑31P anti‑radar missiles and an H‑35 anti‑ship missile from the Black Sea on 15 May (Source: @kpszsu/61910).
Russia reported shooting down 355 drones on the night of 12 May, indicating intense aerial combat (Source: @UkraineNow/81203).
US officials note both sides feel optimistic but stress need for renewed diplomatic mediation (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30172).

points: High volume of Shahed UAVs poses sustained threat to civilian infrastructure across multiple fronts.
Anti‑radar missile launches indicate Russian attempts to suppress Ukrainian air‑defence radars.
Continued missile strikes on Kyiv and Odesa raise the risk of further civilian casualties.
Ukrainian air‑defence successes suggest growing capability to mitigate UAV threats.
Potential escalation if Russia expands ballistic missile use from the south (Source: @kpszsu/61907).
UK pledged faster air‑defence deliveries, enhancing Ukrainian defensive posture.
US diplomatic statements signal willingness to mediate, though both sides remain optimistic about battlefield gains.
Local authorities in Kyiv intensify rescue and de‑brisage operations after missile impact.
Russian regional officials report successful defense of key fuel infrastructure in Ryazan.
Civilian alert systems activated in Odesa and Zaporizhzhia following UAV attacks.
Russian drone‑operator numbers rising to 168,000 by year‑end suggests scaling of UAV warfare.
UAV movement patterns show coordinated attacks from Black Sea toward southern coastal regions.
Multiple sources confirm high UAV kill counts on both sides, indicating a war of attrition in the air.
Russian anti‑radar missile failures may reflect improved Ukrainian electronic warfare.
Increased civilian casualties could influence international support and aid decisions.
